title.rendered: Lo & Graham – Victoria International Marina Wedding

content.rendered:
I recently had the pleasure of photographing the most awesome couple with the coolest style for their wedding. They opted for a fun backyard ceremony, which set the perfect relaxed and intimate atmosphere for their special day. The love between them was undeniable, and it was truly a joy to capture those candid moments of laughter and love.

After the ceremony, we headed over to the Victoria International Marina for their reception, which was nothing short of amazing. The venue provided a stunning backdrop for their celebration, and the live band kept everyone on their feet all night long. The dance floor was packed with guests showing off their best moves, and it was clear that this couple knew how to throw a party!

I feel so grateful to have been a part of such a fun and love-filled day. It’s moments like these that remind me why I love being a photographer.

Vendors

  • Reception Venue: Victoria International Marina
  • Design & Coordination: Your Dream Theme
  • Band: The Bright Side
  • Catering: Taco Revolution
  • Hair: Erin Bradley
  • Makeup: Dani Parkinson
  • Limo: LA Limo
  • Bridal Suite: Spinnakers Guest House
  • Rentals: Pedersons Event Rentals

title.rendered: Katie & Andrew – Intimate Wedding, Cowichan Lake

content.rendered:
I had the pleasure of capturing Katie and Andrew’s intimate wedding, and it was an absolute dream. The festivities took place at Katie’s parents’ stunning property in my hometown of Lake Cowichan. We started off with a first look and some photos at Spring Beach, just a short drive away. The ceremony took place on the charming dock, with the lake as the backdrop. Afterward, we moved on to beautiful long table reception on the upper deck. As the sun began to set, I stole away to newlyweds for some bubbly and quiet moments on the dock. I feel incredibly lucky to have grown up in such a breathtaking place and to have the opportunity to capture these special moments for amazing couples like Katie and Andrew.

  • Makeup: Jessica Pool
  • Flowers: Dancing Dandelion Farms
  • Rentals: Special Occasions
  • Catering: Farm’s Gate

title.rendered: Intimate Wedding – Gorge & HCP Gardens, Victoria

content.rendered:
Remember that heat wave we had back in August? When the stores were sold out of air conditioners and we all sat sweltering in pools of our own sweat for days straight?

I remember gearing myself up for this wedding, making sure I had ample water, lathering on the sun screen, and wearing the most breezy outfit I could find. I was worried about overheating, and looking like a hot sweaty mess… but you know what? All of those worries melted away (😉) as soon as I turned on my camera and captured the first image of the day. I was totally immersed in the magical and unique celebration that was Emily and Indigo’s wedding.

I had been so excited to capture this wedding ever since Emily contacted me back in 2019. Unfortunately, the original 2020 date was postponed due to COVID, but as you will see from the images below, some things are just worth the wait.

This day was so unique to the bride and groom, and so much thought and care went into every detail. A stunning waterfront ceremony, personal vows, ice cream and taco trucks… not to mention the most rad and beautiful couple ever. It truly was the best day.

And, when it was time to wrap up and I snapped out of my photographer trance, I realized that yes, I was quite hot and sticky, and immediately went and jumped in the Gorge. It was the perfect way to end the perfect day!

Vendor Love

  • Ceremony & Reception – Private residence on the Gorge Waterway
  • Bride & Groom Portraits –The Gardens at HCP
  • Flowers – Bespoke Blossoms
  • Cocktails – Twist of Fate
  • Tacos – Taco Revolution
  • Ice Cream – Elements Ice Cream
  • Cake – Ruth & Dean
  • Rentals – Black and White Party Rentals & Shady Spaces
  • Hair – Elevated Hair Salon
  • Makeup – Kreativ Beauty

title.rendered: Miranda & Declan – Intimate Tofino Beach Wedding

content.rendered:
The most beautiful little DIY beach wedding there ever was!

This day was extra special because it was my STUNNING sister-in-law’s. There were a lot of ups and downs when it came to planning and replanning this day due to COVID-19, but in the end, it could not have been more perfect. To top things off, these two were expecting their first baby, so the little peanut (now known as my adorable nephew, Milo) got to be a part of the day! And let’s not forget their sweet pup, Charlie!

The day started with an intimate ceremony, followed by a beach fire, pizza, and a sparkler dance at sunset.

This was my first wedding in Tofino and I hope it won’t be the last.

Location: Middle Beach, Tofino, BC
Florist: Thrifty Foods
Commissioner: Kemal Harris
Music: No Attraction (please go listen, they are amazing)
Bride’s dress: Lulu’s
Bride’s ring: Paul Mara
Groom’s ring: Manly Bands
Pizza: RedCan Gourmet

title.rendered: Kelsey & Steve – Intimate Backyard Wedding, Colwood, BC

content.rendered:
Back in July, I captured the most beautiful, intimate backyard wedding and could not hold back the tears during the first look and vows! Kelsey and Steve have been together since they were teenagers, and their love for each other was so heartening. The first look took place in the forest at Royal Roads University, followed but some portraits at Tower Point and the ceremony took place in the couple’s backyard in Colwood, BC.

  • Photo Locations: Royal Roads University, Tower Point (Witty’s Lagoon), and private backyard residence
  • Catering: Toque Catering
  • Cake: Family
  • Donuts: Empire Donuts
  • Hair/Makeup: Lillian Herner @everystrandmatters
  • Kelsey’s Earrings: Coastal Bead Collective
  • Florist: Kenmar Flower Farm
  • Commissioner: Jennifer Whitehouse
  • Videography: Paper Heart Films
  • Dress: Blush Bridal (consignment)
  • Suit: RW&Co
  • Rings: Paul Mara (custom engagement), People’s Jewellers (bands)

title.rendered: Kara & Jordan – Affinity Guesthouse Wedding

content.rendered:
Kara and Jordan hit it off one night when they crossed paths in a taxi cab. One was about to get out, the other about to get in, and, well, the rest is history. It’s one of my favourite little love stories of all time.

Kara and Jordan’s wedding day was filled with SO much love… I swear everyone in the Cowichan Valley could feel it. K&J have been amazing clients since day one. They are the sweetest, happiest, and most fun couple around, and their wedding day definitely reflected this! I was so lucky to be able to capture it all.

  • Ceremony & Reception Venue: Affinity Guesthouse
  • Catering: Food for Thought
  • Cake: Lighthouse Cake Company
  • Donuts: Sidney Bakery
  • Hair/Makeup: French Twist Bridal
  • Florist: Raspberry Flowers
  • Commissioner: Anne Brunet
  • Videography: Medainy Studio
  • Dress: Truvelle from The White Peony
  • Suit: Moores

title.rendered: Cassandra & Ryan – Birchbank Picnic Grounds Wedding

content.rendered:
Most of the weddings I shoot are local to Vancouver Island. And while I love this beautiful island so much, it is always a pleasure to get asked to capture a wedding elsewhere for a change of scenery. This past July I traveled to the West Kootenays to capture Cassandra and Ryan’s stunning, celestial-themed wedding in Rossland. For those of you who haven’t visited the area, think mountains upon mountains, with stunning valleys and the most picturesque rivers flowing through. The towns in the area are charming, with many heritage buildings and boutique shops… it’s like a scene out of a movie.  

Cassandra and Ryan put so much thought into every little detail of their wedding. The ceremony and reception took place at the Birchbank Picnic Grounds, and the whole place was transformed into a beautiful, starry night. 

As the sun was about to dip below the horizon, we were able to sneak away to the river to capture some blue hour photos. We were lucky to have the bright moon shining down on us. When we returned, Cassandra and Ryan danced to “Harvest Moon” by Neil Young and it couldn’t have been more perfect.

  • Ceremony & Reception Venue: Birchbank Picnic Grounds
  • Hotel: The Josie Hotel
  • Coordination: Wild Smile Events
  • Commissioner: Joan Alexander
  • Ceremony Music: Freya
  • Flowers: Stone Meadow Gardens
  • Catering: Premier Catering
  • Desserts: Starbird Bakehouse
  • DJ: Wyred for Sound Entertainment
  • Hair: Luca Hair & Esthetics
  • Makeup: Alex Hitchcock
  • Bridal Salon: Bliss Bridal
  • Bartender: Teresa Mah

excerpt.rendered: Most of the weddings I shoot are local to Vancouver Island. And while I love this beautiful island to bits, it is always a treat to get asked to capture a wedding elsewhere for a change of scenery. This past July I traveled to the West Kootenays to capture Cassandra and Ryan’s stunning, celestial-themed wedding in Rossland.

title.rendered: Brittany & Logan – Birds Eye Cove Farm Wedding

content.rendered:
Another beautiful Birds Eye Cove Farm wedding in the books! This one was extra special because it was filled with many of my dear friends, including the bride and groom themselves!

I still remember Brittany as an adorable girl who was obsessed with Winnie the Pooh. Brittany and her family are some of the best people I know. They radiate sunshine. You can’t help but smile when they’re around, especially when you hear Sandy, her mom, have a full belly laugh (which she does a lot). It’s contagious.

When the groom, Logan, joined the mix, things just got better. He’s the perfect person for Brittany and such a wonderful, thoughtful person. I could not be happier for the both of them.

Capturing weddings amongst those dearest to me is all that I could ever ask for. But doing it in one at one of the most stunning venues on the island with a rockstar group of vendors is just. the. best.

  • Venue: Birds Eye Cove Farm
  • Event Planning & Coordination: Blue Lily Events
  • Officiant: Rob Filgate
  • Ceremony Music: Simon Vreeswijk
  • Flowers: Night Song Farm
  • Catering: Just Jakes
  • Decor: Fallen Olive
  • Hair: The Ramblerose Hair Studio
  • Makeup: Karley at French Twist Bridal
  • Bride’s Dress: Bliss Gowns & Events
  • Grooms Suit: Indochino

title.rendered: Jessica & Curtis – Beach House Wedding

content.rendered:
I love October. It’s probably my favourite month (although May is a close contender). The light and colours and crisp Autumn air always give me a much needed refresh after a busy summer. It’s the perfect month for a wedding if the weather cooperates, and boy did it cooperate for us! Sunshine all day with a perfect sunset.

Jessica and Curtis had their first look near their house where there was with an abundance of colourful leaves as the backdrop. We spent some time there before heading to the Beach House Restaurant for a stunning oceanside ceremony, followed by sunset portraits at Mount Doug. It was the perfect day.

  • Venue: The Beach House Restaurant
  • Celebrant: Barbara Densmore
  • Ceremony Music: Jill Cooper
  • Flowers: Poppies Floral Art
  • Cake: The Bake House
  • Videography: Whole Hearted Productions
  • DJ: Request the Best
  • Hair: The Ramblerose Hair Studio
  • Makeup: Glam by Lisa
  • Bride’s necklace & earrings: Olive & Piper

title.rendered: Kate & Jake – Goward House & St. Michaels University Wedding

content.rendered:
Kate and Jake’s wedding covered a lot of “firsts” for me as a wedding photographer.

For starters, it was my first time shooting at the beautiful Goward House and St. Michaels University school. The new hall at St. Michaels had just finished construction in time for the wedding, and the floor to ceiling windows and wood beams were swoon-worthy.

Arguably as important, this was the first time I captured a full, 14 person wedding party shotgun, and it was EPIC (I’m pretty sure the bride won… see below).

But the best “first” came a bit later when Jake and his groomsmen DESTROYED the dance floor with their choreographed moves! This mashup of sweet tunes and various hilarious dance moves beat out anything I’d ever seen on YouTube. It was such a fun experience and I’m so glad to have been able to capture it!

All in all, it was a wonderful wedding with such amazing (and FUN) people. I couldn’t have asked for a better day.

  • Venue: Goward House and St. Michaels University School
  • Wedding Coordination: Leah McLachlan
  • Catering: Little Piggy
  • Flowers: Sue-Ann Carter
  • Hair: Niki Lukat
  • Makeup: Andrea Campbell
  • Dress: Made With Love
  • DJ: Keith Thompson Sensational Sound
  • Officiant: Keven Fletcher

title.rendered: Andrea & Matt – Gonzales Observatory Engagement

content.rendered: The Gonzales Observatory sits on a gorgeous hilltop park in Victoria, overlooking the ocean. If it’s a clear day, you can see across the water to the Olympic mountains. It’s a beautiful setting no matter the season, and has been one of my favourite locations for photos this past year. Andrea and Matt met me to do their engagement session back in May (finally getting around to posting now), and we spent some time on the hill during the most gorgeous sunset. Their wedding was also exceptionally beautiful, I promise I’ll get around to sharing some of my favourites from that day too…eventually!

title.rendered: Jenna & Jordan – Bird’s Eye Cove Farm Wedding

content.rendered:
Jenna & Jordan are the nicest, most down-to-earth couple and shooting their wedding was a dream come true. I felt right at home with their family and friends—everyone was so happy to be there and the vibe was fun and energetic all day long. J & J decided to do their first look, family and bridal portraits before the ceremony, which meant they could join in cocktail hour afterwards and spend lots of quality time with their guests. This worked out perfectly, and the cocktail hour was so fun as a result.

I love shooting weddings at Bird’s Eye Cove Farm. It’s funny, because both times I have worked there, it has rained pretty hard, resulting in the ceremony being moved inside last minute. Luckily the venue is just as gorgeous inside as it is outside, so no problems there! Shoutout to Tammy from Blue Lily Events for being so calm and organized and making what could be a hectic situation into a breeze.

Fun fact: This was my first wedding where the guests applauded as the bride came down the aisle, and it was so fun and adorable, I almost died.

Fun fact #2 : The photo of the wedding party standing against the barn windows was a finalist at the Vancouver Island Wedding Awards, it was an honour to be recognized amongst all of the talent we have on the island.

Thanks to J+J and their fantastic family and friends for such an amazing day!

  • Venue: Bird’s Eye Cove Farm
  • Wedding Coordination: Blue Lily Events
  • Catering: The London Chef
  • Flowers: Kenmar Flower Farm
  • Makeup: AV Makeup Artistry
  • Dress: Ladybelle Bridal
  • DJ: Hey Mr. DJ
  • Commissioner: Lura McCallum

title.rendered: Emily & Sean – Victoria Golf Club Wedding

content.rendered:
“Basically if you’re not taking our photos we will be delaying our wedding to 2018 so that you can. Yes we are nuts.”

Sometimes you get clients that make you feel like a million bucks.

If you’ve looked through my older posts, you might recognize this gorgeous bride, Emily, from when she was Maid of Honour in her sister’s wedding in 2015. Emily contacted me the following year to book me for her wedding, and at the time I was in the early stages of planning a trip to Europe for when they were hoping to book… but how could I say no?

Long story short : I went to Europe in May instead, and am so glad that I did. I had the most amazing time shooting these beautiful humans, and they were nothing but wonderful throughout the whole process.

Fun fact: Emily and Sean’s dog Ruth was a bridesmaid, and boy was she ever cute.

  • Venue & Catering: Victoria Golf Club
  • Wedding Coordination & Styling: Rock Paper Square
  • Cake: Ruth & Dean
  • Hair: Elena of Conscious Hair and Maia of Wiink2
  • Flowers: Thorn & Thistle
  • Makeup: Alexa & Laura of Artistry by Alexa
  • DJ: Levi Hawk
  • Officiant: Sherry Lynne Easthom

title.rendered: Leslee & Kenny – Roundhouse Farm Wedding

content.rendered:
Leslee and Kenny’s wedding was anything but typical and such fun way to spend a summer’s day! Being music lovers and festival-goers, the couple worked with some amazing vendors to pull off a celebration that really showcased who they are as a couple. Great music, drool-worthy eats (including a food truck and oyster bar), florals, drinks, games, and so many cute nooks to hang out in — this wedding was one for the books! Please check out the list of all of the amazing vendors below.

Fun fact : Since their wedding fell on a long weekend, the couple invited all of their friends who weren’t able to join in on the smaller wedding to a festival-inspired private party at Lucky Bar the following night. How cool is that?!

  • Venue: Roundhouse Farm
  • Wedding Coordination: Island Born Events 
  • Catering: The Melt Truck, The Wandering Mollusk
  • Cake: Ruth & Dean
  • Donuts: Empire Donuts
  • Flowers: Powell Floral Design
  • Custom candles for party favours : Milk Jar Candle Co. (Leslee’s sister & MOH makes these, they smelled so good!)
  • Hair & Makeup: Tarah Gatsi of Ten Fifteen Beauty
  • DJ:Jeff Lingwood of Party People Sound Solutions
  • Bar and Bartender: Magical Moments Decorating
  • Officiant – Teresa Kojder

title.rendered: Cara & Shaun – English Inn Wedding

content.rendered:
Cara & Shaun’s wedding had so much turn-of-the-century charm. The English Inn was the perfect setting for the Downton Abbey-esque theme, and the groom and groomsmen wore ascot ties and tailcoats to boot! And to top it all off, the newlyweds were chauffeured around in a vintage car. So many little details made this wedding such a treat to capture, and Cara & Shaun are one of the sweetest couples you’ll ever meet.

  • Venue: English Inn
  • Catering: Truffles Catering
  • Flowers: Foxgloves Flowers
  • Cake : Belle’s Confections
  • Hair/Makeup: Heartwood & Co. 
  • Dress: Ladybelle Bridal
  • Suits: Moores
  • DJ: Whitelotus Events
  • Commissioner: Cathy Lavoie
  • Vintage Car : Classic Car Tours

title.rendered: Allision & Craig – Church & State Wedding

content.rendered:
When Allison and Craig asked me to capture their wedding at Church & State, I got excited. If you know me, you know I love wine, and having only ever been a guest at the venue, I had been waiting for an opportunity to shoot at this pretty little winery. It was the perfect day filled with wonderful people. I really really love when children get to be a part of their parent’s wedding day. Allison and Craig’s daughter made the best flower girl, and had a first dance with her dad to “a whole new world” from Aladdin (and yes, I know every word)! Also, Allision and her dad rocked out to “Love Shack” which was just… groovy.

  • Venue/Catering: Church & State Wines
  • Flowers: Thrifty Food’s Broadmead
  • Cake : Schur to Please
  • Makeup: Hailey J. 
  • Hair: Caryn Chartier and Emma Illsley
  • DJ: Cam, Music Masters
  • Dress : Shades of White Bridal
  • Suits : Moores
  • Commissioner: Iain Hooey

title.rendered: Thea & Greg – Tofino Engagement

content.rendered:
Mackenzie Beach, Tofino BC

There really isn’t a better way to spend the first day of the new year than on the beach with your best friend and her new fiance. Tofino is one of my favourite places, and the winter light was divine (we were lucky to have sun all weekend). At the end of the shoot I asked if Greg would snap a photo of me and Thea, and as he took it she asked me to be her Maid of Honour. It was a pretty special moment (check out the last frame to see for yourselves)!

The year ahead is bound to be bright and shiny, and I can’t wait to stand up with my best friend as she marries the love of her life.

xoxo

title.rendered: Paul & Rachel – Fairburn Guesthouse Wedding

content.rendered:
Fairburn Guesthouse, Duncan BC

Paul and Rachel’s wedding was intimate and stunningly beautiful. The ceremony and reception were both designed with such careful detail by the team at ma-luxe, and Fairburn Farm is indescribably beautiful (just look at the photos)! Corn fields, rolling hills, a rustic barn and water buffalo! If you haven’t had the chance to meet a water buffalo yet, you should put it on your list, they are the cutest animals ever, and love eating maple leaves (who woulda thought?)

I adore shooting small weddings because it gives me the chance to meet everyone, and even make a few new friends on occasion! You know it’s been a good one when you say good-bye to the bride and groom and they are still wearing photo booth props from an hour ago.

Vendors  

  • Ceremony & Reception Venue | Fairburn Guesthouse

title.rendered: Melissa & Dave – Starling Lane Wedding

content.rendered:
Starling Lane Vineyard & Prospect Lake Hall

Melissa and Dave’s wedding was beautiful, crazy, and full of surprises. After a gorgeous ceremony at Starling Lane Winery, the winds were strong enough to knock the power out at the reception hall, and I have never seen so many awesome people rally together to come up with an electricity solution without any loss of time. Kudos to all of the wonderful people at Melissa & Dave’s wedding, and an even bigger kudos to this amazing couple who kept on smiling regardless of all of the surprises thrown their way. Wishing you two so many years of happiness! You truly deserve it.

title.rendered: Jeff & Allison – Bird’s Eye Cove Farm Wedding

content.rendered:
Jeff and Allison were married at the beautiful Birds Eye Cove Farm, which is a highly recommended spot to visit if you haven’t been (they do pizza nights every Monday, yum!) I had never worked at this location before, so was thrilled to have so much scenery at my disposal.

Just as the ceremony was about to begin, we were greeted with a heavy dose of rain, and the outdoor wedding had to become an indoor wedding in a matter of minutes. Luckily the inside of the venue is equally as beautiful as the outside, with big windows overlooking the property, so aside from the quick switcheroo, everything went off without a hitch.  Jeff and Allison were a dream to work with, and the wedding was as sweet as can be.

And,  if it weren’t for the rain we would not have had a glorious rainbow appear out of nowhere. How can you be upset about that?

Thank you Allison and Jeff for an amazing day. Your wedding was truly wonderful. And a big thanks to Bird’s Eye Cove Farm for the fantastic backdrop.

The photo you see above was awarded “Best Duo Photograph” at the 2015 Vancouver Island Wedding Awards. What an honour!

Vendors  

  • Venue & Catering | Bird’s Eye Cove Farm
  • Event Planning | Blue Lily Events
  • Hair and Makeup | As You Wish Beauty
  • Dress | Ladybelle Bridal
  • DJ | KGDJ

title.rendered: Ben & Rose – Backyard Wedding

content.rendered:
Backyard Wedding, Victoria BC

There’s something so special about small weddings, especially when the bride and groom are your close friends.

Ben and Rose were married in late July in Rose’s mom’s backyard. With a total of 50 guests, and familiar, friendly faces everywhere, the day was filled with a sense of togetherness. The day went off without a hitch. There were so many unique elements that made it extra memorable — a puppy ring bearer named Betsy, beautiful mismatched China table settings, foosball, delicious home-cooked entrees and baked treats … the list goes on and on.

When the night was over, I remember thinking how I wish we could to go back in time and do it all over again. Thank god for memories, moments captured, and friends that will last a life time.

excerpt.rendered: There’s something so special about small weddings, especially when the bride and groom are your close friends…

title.rendered: Dan & Heidi – Avalon Guest House Wedding

content.rendered:
Avalon Guest House, Duncan BC

The little details that went into Heidi and Dan’s wedding were nothing short of phenomenal. When I walked into the barn that morning I was blown away. Looking around, it was pretty clear how much thought and hard work had gone into it all.

The ceremony was beautiful – in true country fashion, guests sat out on hay bales, overlooking rolling fields with horses grazing in the distance. There were cowboy boots a-plenty, and with both Heidi and Dan coming from small towns, everybody knew everybody. No need for ice-breaking, it was warm all day long!

Up the road from the farm was a field with a lone, beautiful old tree. A few days before the wedding, Heidi told me how she had loved this tree since she was a little girl and would love to take some photos there. When I went to scope the location the day before, a local came up to me and began to tell me how the city had just bought the land and was going to turn the field into the new police station. I feel lucky to have captured such a special place before it was gone forever.

title.rendered: Nicole & Greg – Glencoe Cove & Beach House Wedding

content.rendered:
Glencoe Cove + Beach House Restaurant, Victoria BC

Back in the summer of last year, Nicole and Greg contacted me to take some photos of their new family. Their son, Silas, was 2 months at the time, and cute as a button. I had a great time shooting the three — they were so easy going (including Silas, which at 2 months, is a true sign of character!) and clearly very much in love. Naturally, I was extremely flattered when they asked me to capture their wedding shortly after.

This was my first wedding of the 2014 season, and it was beautiful. The ceremony took place on the bluffs overlooking the Haro Strait at Glencoe Cove Park, followed by the reception at the fantastic Beach House restaurant in Cordova Bay.
